chapter_goal: Turn the space of compact metric spaces into an inspectable object by comparing finite distance data, correspondences, epsilon-isometries, and length-space approximations.

visual_sequence.inspection_target: which features survive under blow-up, shrink-down, or GH comparison
relative versus absolute changes in distance matrices
the worst pair-pair error controls the GH estimate
net radius tends to zero while diameter remains controlled

visual_sequence.concept: compact length spaces as graph limits
visual_sequence.inspection_target: graph shortest-path metrics approximate sampled length distances
where correspondences and midpoint criterion enter
change n and read the distance matrix and pi/n bound

libraries_used.why: finite metric matrices, brute-force tiny correspondences, convergence checks
durable static diagrams and heatmaps for metric data
small standalone slider lab without extra runtime dependencies
